title: X.509, PKI, Trust Stores, and Certificate Validation · Execution Trace: Crypto Go X509 Structure
summary: An internal client accepts any certificate signed by a familiar CA, skips hostname checks, ignores key usage and time, and assumes revocation always works online. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the x509 structure increment in certificate path validation and issuance profile. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Parse certificates, names, SANs, key usages, extensions, serials, validity, signatures, and chains without treating parsed data as trusted.
Inventory affected uses, contain authority, rotate or revoke, rewrap or re-encrypt, restore service, notify stakeholders, and prove old-key handling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go X509 Structure · Theory Crypto Go X509 Structure
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 7443 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a bounded X.509 path against name, time, usage, trust-anchor, and explicit revocation policy; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
